title: Tripleurospermum transcaucasicum
taxonomy: Tripleurospermum transcaucasicum is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Tripleurospermum, which includes several species of dandelion-like plants. The genus Tripleurospermum is closely related to the genus Hypochaeris and is often included in the same tribe, Senecioneae. However, the exact classification of Tripleurospermum transcaucasicum may vary depending on the source and the latest taxonomic research. It is native to the Caucasus region, which is a mountain system at the border between Europe and Asia.

intro: Tripleurospermum transcaucasicum, also known as Caucasian knawel, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family, native to the Caucasus region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate conditions such as arthritis and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
